title: Telling a Story
cefrDescriptors: Can narrate a past experience, distinguishing background from events.
Can situate events in time with il y a and pendant.
concept:
In A1 you met the passé composé and the imparfait separately. Now you'll use them TOGETHER to tell a story — the heart of narration in French.

The division of labour:
  • imparfait = the SCENE and background: weather, feelings, what was going on, descriptions.
      Il faisait beau. J'étais au café. Elle portait une robe rouge.
  • passé composé = the EVENTS that move the story forward, one after another.
      J'ai vu Léa. On a parlé. Elle a souri.
Think of the imparfait as the film set, and the passé composé as the actions filmed on it.

A few irregular past participles you'll keep needing: voir → vu, faire → fait, être → été, avoir → eu, prendre → pris, dire → dit.

To place events in time, use these markers:
  • il y a + duration = AGO → « il y a deux ans » (two years ago).
  • pendant + duration = FOR / DURING → « pendant une heure » (for an hour).
  (dans + duration points to the future: « dans deux jours » = in two days.)

The theme is the classic French story — la rencontre (the meeting): rencontrer (to meet), le coup de foudre (love at first sight), sourire (to smile), un regard (a look), tout de suite (right away).

Put it together: « J'ai rencontré Léa il y a deux ans. Il faisait beau, on a parlé pendant des heures… c'était le coup de foudre ! »

Tell a 3-sentence story of how you met a friend: set the scene (imparfait), then two things that happened (passé composé).

vocabulary.exampleEn: It was love at first sight.
I like the way he/she looks.

vocabulary.exampleFr: On a parlé pendant une heure.

examples.fr: J'ai rencontré Léa il y a deux ans.
examples.en: I met Léa two years ago.

examples.fr: Il faisait beau et j'étais au café.
examples.en: The weather was nice and I was at the café.

examples.fr: Quand j'ai vu Léa, j'ai tout de suite souri.
examples.en: When I saw Léa, I smiled right away.
It was love at first sight!

examples.fr: On a parlé pendant des heures.
